aws.name: Root account access keys disabled
aws.expected: AccountAccessKeysPresent == 0
aws.remediation: Delete root account access keys via IAM console.

aws.name: IAM users have active credentials reviewed
aws.expected: All active users have logged in within 90 days or credentials are deactivated
aws.remediation: Remove or deactivate unused IAM user accounts.

aws.name: IAM password policy enforced
aws.expected: Password policy exists with minimum length >= 14
aws.remediation: Configure IAM password policy with minimum 14 characters.

azure.name: Conditional Access policies configured
azure.expected: At least one active Conditional Access policy exists
azure.remediation: Configure Conditional Access policies in Azure AD.

azure.name: Guest user access restricted
azure.expected: Guest user access is restricted to properties of their own objects
azure.remediation: Restrict guest user permissions in Azure AD external collaboration settings.

azure.name: Security defaults or Conditional Access enabled
azure.expected: Security defaults enabled or Conditional Access policies deployed
azure.remediation: Enable Security Defaults or deploy Conditional Access policies.

gcp.name: Organization-level IAM bindings reviewed
gcp.expected: No overly permissive bindings (allUsers, allAuthenticatedUsers)
gcp.remediation: Remove public IAM bindings from organization/project.

gcp.name: Service account keys rotated
gcp.expected: No service account keys older than 90 days
gcp.remediation: Rotate service account keys older than 90 days.

gcp.name: Default service account not used
gcp.expected: No compute instances use the default service account
gcp.remediation: Create dedicated service accounts for compute instances.

aws.name: IAM policies follow least privilege
aws.expected: No policies with Action:* and Resource:* on customer-managed policies
aws.remediation: Replace wildcard permissions with specific actions and resources.

aws.name: IAM permission boundaries configured for delegated admin
aws.expected: Users with administrative delegation have permission boundaries attached
aws.remediation: Attach permission boundaries to delegated administrator IAM users.

azure.name: Custom RBAC roles use least privilege
azure.expected: No custom roles with wildcard actions (*/*)
azure.remediation: Update custom RBAC roles to use specific actions instead of wildcards.

azure.name: PIM enabled for privileged roles
azure.expected: Privileged Identity Management is enabled for Global Admin and other critical roles
azure.remediation: Enable Azure AD Privileged Identity Management for administrative roles.

gcp.name: Custom IAM roles scoped appropriately
gcp.expected: Custom roles do not include overly broad permissions
gcp.remediation: Review and scope down custom IAM role permissions.

gcp.name: Primitive roles not assigned to users
gcp.expected: No users have roles/owner, roles/editor at project level
gcp.remediation: Replace primitive roles (Owner, Editor) with predefined or custom roles.

aws.name: VPC Flow Logs enabled
aws.expected: Flow logs enabled for all VPCs
aws.remediation: Enable VPC Flow Logs for all VPCs in ACCEPT and REJECT mode.

aws.name: S3 Block Public Access enabled at account level
aws.expected: All four public access block settings enabled at account level
aws.remediation: Enable S3 Block Public Access at the account level.

azure.name: NSG flow logs enabled
azure.expected: Flow logs enabled for all NSGs
azure.remediation: Enable NSG flow logs for all Network Security Groups.

azure.name: Azure Firewall or Network Virtual Appliance deployed
azure.expected: At least one Azure Firewall or NVA is deployed for traffic inspection
azure.remediation: Deploy Azure Firewall or approved Network Virtual Appliance for CUI traffic inspection.

gcp.name: VPC Flow Logs enabled
gcp.expected: Flow logs enabled on all subnets
gcp.remediation: Enable VPC Flow Logs on all subnets.

gcp.name: Firewall rules reviewed for least privilege
gcp.expected: No firewall rules allow 0.0.0.0/0 ingress to all ports
gcp.remediation: Restrict firewall rules to specific ports and source ranges.

objectives_requiring_documentation.text: designated sources and destinations (e.g., networks, individuals, and devices) for CUI within systems and between interconnected systems are identified.
objectives_requiring_documentation.evidence_needed: Provide documentation showing that designated sources and destinations (e.g., networks, individuals, and devices) for cui within systems and between interconnected systems are identified and documented.

aws.name: Separate IAM roles for admin and operational tasks
aws.expected: Administrative roles are separate from operational/developer roles
aws.remediation: Create separate IAM roles for administrative and operational functions.

aws.name: No single user has both deploy and approve permissions
aws.expected: No user/role has both CodePipeline:ApproveManualApproval and CodeDeploy:CreateDeployment
aws.remediation: Separate deployment approval and execution permissions across different roles.

azure.name: Separation of duties for subscription management
azure.expected: Owner and User Access Administrator roles assigned to different individuals
azure.remediation: Assign Owner and User Access Administrator roles to different individuals.

gcp.name: Separation of duties for project management
gcp.expected: No single user has both roles/iam.admin and roles/owner
gcp.remediation: Separate IAM admin and project owner roles across different principals.

aws.name: No inline IAM policies with wildcard permissions
aws.expected: No inline policies with Action:* and Resource:*
aws.remediation: Replace inline wildcard policies with scoped managed policies.

aws.name: IAM Access Analyzer enabled
aws.expected: At least one active IAM Access Analyzer in each region
aws.remediation: Enable IAM Access Analyzer in all active regions.

aws.name: No IAM users with AdministratorAccess policy
aws.expected: AdministratorAccess not attached directly to IAM users
aws.remediation: Use IAM roles with time-limited sessions instead of direct admin user access.

azure.name: Global Administrator role limited
azure.expected: Five or fewer Global Administrator accounts
azure.remediation: Reduce the number of Global Administrator accounts to five or fewer.

azure.name: JIT VM access configured
azure.expected: JIT access configured for management ports on all VMs
azure.remediation: Enable Just-In-Time VM access in Microsoft Defender for Cloud.

gcp.name: No user has Owner role on multiple projects
gcp.expected: Owner role is limited to break-glass accounts only
gcp.remediation: Remove Owner role from regular users; use predefined roles instead.

gcp.name: IAM recommender reviewed
gcp.expected: No outstanding IAM recommendations for permission reduction
gcp.remediation: Review and apply IAM Recommender suggestions to reduce excess permissions.

aws.name: Separate admin and standard user roles defined
aws.expected: Distinct roles exist for administrative and non-administrative functions
aws.remediation: Create separate IAM roles for privileged and non-privileged access.

azure.name: Admin accounts separate from daily-use accounts
azure.expected: Administrative users have separate admin-only accounts
azure.remediation: Create dedicated admin accounts separate from daily-use accounts.

gcp.name: Admin and user roles separated
gcp.expected: Users with admin roles do not use those roles for daily non-admin tasks
gcp.remediation: Create separate identities for administrative and standard activities.

aws.name: CloudTrail logging enabled for all management events
aws.expected: At least one multi-region trail logging all management events
aws.remediation: Enable a multi-region CloudTrail trail with management events.

aws.name: SCP prevents disabling CloudTrail
aws.expected: SCP exists preventing cloudtrail:StopLogging and cloudtrail:DeleteTrail
aws.remediation: Create an SCP that denies cloudtrail:StopLogging and cloudtrail:DeleteTrail.

azure.name: Activity Log alerts for privilege escalation
azure.expected: Alerts configured for role assignment creation and policy changes
azure.remediation: Configure Activity Log alerts for administrative privilege changes.

gcp.name: Admin Activity audit logs enabled
gcp.expected: Admin Activity audit logs are always enabled (cannot be disabled)
gcp.remediation: Verify Admin Activity audit logs are active (enabled by default).

gcp.name: Log-based metrics and alerts for IAM changes
gcp.expected: Alert policies exist for IAM policy changes
gcp.remediation: Create log-based metrics and alerts for IAM policy modifications.

aws.name: Account lockout policy configured in IAM Identity Center
aws.expected: Account lockout configured after 5 failed attempts
aws.remediation: Configure account lockout policy in IAM Identity Center.

aws.name: GuardDuty brute force finding type enabled
aws.expected: GuardDuty enabled and monitoring for brute force attacks
aws.remediation: Enable GuardDuty and verify brute force finding types are active.

azure.name: Smart lockout configured in Azure AD
azure.expected: Smart lockout threshold is 10 or fewer attempts
azure.remediation: Configure Azure AD Smart Lockout with appropriate thresholds.

gcp.name: Google Workspace login challenge enabled
gcp.expected: Login challenges configured for suspicious activity
gcp.remediation: Enable login challenges in Google Workspace admin console.

AC-8.manual_guidance: Verify system use notification banners are configured on all CUI systems. Check login banners include required language about authorized use, monitoring, and consent to recording.

objectives_requiring_documentation.text: privacy and security notices required by CUI-specified rules are identified, consistent, and associated with the specific CUI category
objectives_requiring_documentation.evidence_needed: Provide documentation showing that privacy and security notices required by cui-specified rules are identified and documented.

objectives_requiring_documentation.text: privacy and security notices are displayed.
objectives_requiring_documentation.evidence_needed: Provide documentation or process evidence: privacy and security notices are displayed.

aws.name: Console session timeout configured
aws.expected: MaxSessionDuration set to 3600 seconds (1 hour) or less for console roles
aws.remediation: Set maximum session duration to 1 hour or less for console access roles.

azure.name: Conditional Access session controls configured
azure.expected: Session lifetime policies enforce re-authentication after inactivity
azure.remediation: Configure Conditional Access session controls with sign-in frequency.

gcp.name: Session control policy configured
gcp.expected: Session re-authentication required after defined inactivity period
gcp.remediation: Configure session control policies in Google BeyondCorp Enterprise.

aws.name: IAM role session duration limited
aws.expected: MaxSessionDuration for all roles is 3600 seconds or less
aws.remediation: Reduce MaxSessionDuration on IAM roles to 1 hour or less.

aws.name: SSO session timeout configured
aws.expected: Session duration configured with appropriate timeout
aws.remediation: Configure SSO session timeout in IAM Identity Center.

azure.name: Token lifetime policy configured
azure.expected: Access token lifetime is 1 hour or less
azure.remediation: Configure token lifetime policies with appropriate access token lifetimes.

gcp.name: OAuth token expiration configured
gcp.expected: OAuth token lifetimes are appropriately limited
gcp.remediation: Configure appropriate token expiration for service accounts and user sessions.

aws.name: VPN connections use CloudWatch monitoring
aws.expected: VPN connections have CloudWatch metrics and logging enabled
aws.remediation: Enable CloudWatch monitoring for all VPN connections.

aws.name: Systems Manager Session Manager logging enabled
aws.expected: Session Manager configured to log sessions to S3 and CloudWatch
aws.remediation: Configure Session Manager to log all sessions to S3 and CloudWatch Logs.

azure.name: Azure Bastion deployed for remote access
azure.expected: Azure Bastion deployed for secure remote access to VMs
azure.remediation: Deploy Azure Bastion for secure remote access to virtual machines.

gcp.name: IAP for TCP forwarding enabled
gcp.expected: Identity-Aware Proxy TCP forwarding used for remote access
gcp.remediation: Enable IAP TCP forwarding for secure remote access to instances.

aws.name: VPN uses approved encryption
aws.expected: VPN connections use AES-256-GCM or AES-256-CBC encryption
aws.remediation: Configure VPN connections with AES-256 encryption algorithms.

aws.name: TLS 1.2+ enforced on all load balancers
aws.expected: All load balancers use TLS 1.2 or higher security policies
aws.remediation: Update load balancer listeners to use TLS 1.2+ security policies.

azure.name: VPN Gateway uses IKEv2 with strong encryption
azure.expected: VPN connections use IKEv2 with AES-256 encryption
azure.remediation: Configure VPN Gateway connections with IKEv2 and AES-256 encryption.

gcp.name: Cloud VPN uses IKEv2 with strong ciphers
gcp.expected: VPN tunnels use IKEv2 with AES-256 encryption
gcp.remediation: Configure Cloud VPN tunnels with IKEv2 and AES-256 cipher suites.

aws.name: Client VPN endpoint configured
aws.expected: Client VPN endpoints exist for remote access with centralized logging
aws.remediation: Deploy AWS Client VPN for managed remote access control points.

aws.name: No direct SSH/RDP access from internet
aws.expected: No security groups allow inbound SSH (22) or RDP (3389) from 0.0.0.0/0
aws.remediation: Remove direct SSH/RDP access from internet; use bastion hosts or Session Manager.

azure.name: No direct RDP/SSH from internet
azure.expected: No NSG rules allow inbound RDP (3389) or SSH (22) from Internet
azure.remediation: Remove direct RDP/SSH access; use Azure Bastion for remote management.

gcp.name: No direct SSH from internet via firewall rules
gcp.expected: No firewall rules allow SSH (22) from 0.0.0.0/0
gcp.remediation: Remove firewall rules allowing SSH from internet; use IAP tunneling.

aws.name: Session Manager used for privileged remote access
aws.expected: SSM Agent installed and Session Manager used for remote admin
aws.remediation: Install SSM Agent and use Session Manager for remote privileged access.

azure.name: Privileged Access Workstation policy enforced
azure.expected: Conditional Access requires compliant devices for admin access
azure.remediation: Configure Conditional Access to require compliant/managed devices for admin roles.

gcp.name: OS Login enabled for privileged access
gcp.expected: OS Login is enabled at the project level for instance access management
gcp.remediation: Enable OS Login at the project level for centralized access control.

aws.name: VPN infrastructure for remote access authorization
aws.expected: Site-to-site VPN connections or Client VPN endpoints configured for controlled remote access
aws.remediation: Deploy AWS Site-to-Site VPN or Client VPN endpoints to control and authorize remote access.

azure.name: VPN gateway for remote access authorization
azure.expected: VPN gateways configured for controlled remote access
azure.remediation: Deploy Azure VPN Gateway to control and authorize remote access connections.

gcp.name: Cloud VPN for remote access authorization
gcp.expected: Cloud VPN gateways and tunnels configured for controlled remote access
gcp.remediation: Deploy Cloud VPN gateways and tunnels to control and authorize remote access.

objectives_requiring_documentation.evidence_needed: Provide documentation showing that wireless access points are identified and documented.

objectives_requiring_documentation.text: wireless access is authorized prior to allowing such connections.
objectives_requiring_documentation.evidence_needed: Provide documentation or process evidence: wireless access is authorized prior to allowing such connections.

AC-19(5).manual_guidance: Verify wireless networks use WPA2/WPA3-Enterprise with 802.1X authentication and FIPS 140-2 validated encryption. Check that wireless infrastructure is configured according to organizational security standards.

objectives_requiring_documentation.text: wireless access to the system is protected using encryption.
objectives_requiring_documentation.evidence_needed: Provide documentation or process evidence: wireless access to the system is protected using encryption.

objectives_requiring_documentation.text: wireless access to the system is protected using authentication.
objectives_requiring_documentation.evidence_needed: Provide documentation or process evidence: wireless access to the system is protected using authentication.

aws.name: Centralized identity providers for device control
aws.expected: SAML or OIDC identity providers configured for centralized authentication and device control
aws.remediation: Configure SAML or OIDC identity providers in IAM for centralized identity and device management.

azure.name: Defender for Cloud and managed identity for device control
azure.expected: Microsoft Defender for Cloud enabled and managed identity role assignments present
azure.remediation: Enable Microsoft Defender for Cloud and use managed identities for centralized device and identity management.

gcp.name: OS Login and device security org policies
gcp.expected: At least 2 of 3 device security org policies enforced: OS Login, serial port disabled, shielded VM required
gcp.remediation: Enforce org policy constraints: compute.requireOsLogin, compute.disableSerialPortAccess, compute.requireShieldedVm.

objectives_requiring_documentation.text: mobile devices that process, store, or transmit CUI are identified.
objectives_requiring_documentation.evidence_needed: Provide documentation showing that mobile devices that process, store, or transmit cui are identified and documented.

objectives_requiring_documentation.text: the connection of mobile devices is authorized.
objectives_requiring_documentation.evidence_needed: Provide documentation or process evidence: the connection of mobile devices is authorized.

objectives_requiring_documentation.text: mobile device connections are monitored and logged.
objectives_requiring_documentation.evidence_needed: Provide documentation or process evidence: mobile device connections are monitored and logged.

aws.name: EBS default encryption enabled for compute platforms
aws.expected: EBS encryption by default is enabled in the account
aws.remediation: Enable EBS encryption by default to ensure all new EBS volumes are automatically encrypted.

azure.name: All managed disks encrypted
azure.expected: All managed disks have encryption enabled (platform-managed or customer-managed keys)
azure.remediation: Enable disk encryption on all managed disks using Azure Disk Encryption or server-side encryption.

gcp.name: CMEK org policy or disk-level CMEK enforced
gcp.expected: CMEK org policy enforced, or all compute disks use customer-managed encryption keys
gcp.remediation: Enforce CMEK via org policy constraint or ensure all Compute Engine disks use CMEK from Cloud KMS.

objectives_requiring_documentation.text: mobile devices and mobile computing platforms that process, store, or transmit CUI are identified.
objectives_requiring_documentation.evidence_needed: Provide documentation showing that mobile devices and mobile computing platforms that process, store, or transmit cui are identified and documented.

objectives_requiring_documentation.text: encryption is employed to protect CUI on identified mobile devices and mobile computing platforms.
objectives_requiring_documentation.evidence_needed: Provide documentation or process evidence: encryption is employed to protect CUI on identified mobile devices and mobile computing platforms.

aws.name: VPC peering connections reviewed
aws.expected: All VPC peering connections are documented and authorized
aws.remediation: Review and document all VPC peering connections; remove unauthorized ones.

aws.name: Transit Gateway attachments reviewed
aws.expected: All Transit Gateway attachments are authorized and documented
aws.remediation: Review Transit Gateway attachments and remove unauthorized connections.

azure.name: VNet peering connections reviewed
azure.expected: All VNet peering connections are documented and authorized
azure.remediation: Review and document all VNet peering connections; remove unauthorized ones.

gcp.name: VPC peering connections reviewed
gcp.expected: All VPC peering connections are authorized and documented
gcp.remediation: Review all VPC Network Peering connections; remove unauthorized ones.

aws.name: S3 Block Public Access enabled at account level
aws.expected: All four S3 Block Public Access settings enabled at account level
aws.remediation: Enable all four S3 Block Public Access settings at the account level to prevent public exposure of stored data.

azure.name: Storage accounts block public blob access
azure.expected: AllowBlobPublicAccess is false on all storage accounts
azure.remediation: Disable public blob access on all storage accounts to prevent unauthorized data exposure.

gcp.name: Uniform bucket-level access org policy enforced
gcp.expected: uniformBucketLevelAccess org policy constraint enforced to prevent per-object ACLs
gcp.remediation: Enforce the storage.uniformBucketLevelAccess org policy constraint to prevent fine-grained ACLs on Cloud Storage.

objectives_requiring_documentation.text: use of organizational portable storage devices containing CUI on external systems is identified and documented.
objectives_requiring_documentation.evidence_needed: Provide documentation showing that use of organizational portable storage devices containing cui on external systems are identified and documented.

objectives_requiring_documentation.text: limits on the use of organizational portable storage devices containing CUI on external systems are defined.
objectives_requiring_documentation.evidence_needed: Provide documentation showing that limits on the use of organizational portable storage devices containing cui on external systems are defined.

objectives_requiring_documentation.text: use of organizational portable storage devices containing CUI on external systems is limited as defined.
objectives_requiring_documentation.evidence_needed: Provide documentation or process evidence: use of organizational portable storage devices containing CUI on external systems is limited as defined.

aws.name: No S3 buckets publicly accessible
aws.expected: No S3 buckets have IsPublic=true
aws.remediation: Enable S3 Block Public Access and review bucket policies for public access.

aws.name: No EC2 instances with public IPs in CUI subnets
aws.expected: No instances in CUI-designated subnets have public IP addresses
aws.remediation: Remove public IPs from instances in CUI subnets; use NAT gateway for outbound.

azure.name: No storage accounts with public blob access
azure.expected: AllowBlobPublicAccess is false on all storage accounts
azure.remediation: Disable public blob access on all storage accounts.

gcp.name: No Cloud Storage buckets publicly accessible
gcp.expected: No buckets have allUsers or allAuthenticatedUsers bindings
gcp.remediation: Remove public access bindings from Cloud Storage buckets.
